body:
CONFIRMED BY RUNNING IT (2026-07-21). `test/gateway/promote-survival-probe.test.ts` reproduces it. Found during the T38 fallout sweep - same family, DIFFERENT REMEDY, which is why it is its own ticket.

WHAT HAPPENS. `promoteImpl` (src/gateway/adopt.ts) gates on presence:

    const src = source.reactor.get(deltaId);
    if (src === undefined) throw new Error(`nothing to promote: ...`);

`reactor.get` says the delta EXISTS in the pool. It says nothing about whether it SURVIVES there - whether something in the pool struck it. Meanwhile `promotionRefusal` correctly refuses to promote a NEGATION ('a retraction is the operator's own §14 act, never an adopted output').

Each rule is right alone; together they leave a hole. The retraction cannot cross, but its target can. So a sandboxed app that produces an interpretation and then WITHDRAWS it leaves an output the operator can still adopt - and promotion RE-SIGNS, so the withdrawn claim enters the primary in the OPERATOR'S OWN VOICE with full force. The record then shows the operator asserting something its author had already taken back, with a `loam.adoption` trail that makes it look properly sourced.

WHY THIS IS WORSE THAN T38. T38's seeding edge gave a POOL a wrong reading of the primary - bad, but contained behind glass and dropped with the pool. This puts a resurrected claim into CANONICAL history under the operator's signature, where §11 erasure is the only way back out. Promotion is the one door out of the quarantine (§24.9's 'promotion is the only crossing'), so it is exactly the door that must not pass something the pool had already struck.

FIX (recommendation): `promoteImpl` refuses a source delta that does not SURVIVE in the source. Reuse the pool's own negation reading rather than inventing a second notion of survival - whatever `lawfulNegated`/the reactor already answers for 'is this struck', ask that. The refusal must NAME the retraction (the codebase's refusals say why), and it belongs beside the existing `promotionRefusal` checks so the whole promotion gate reads in one place.

Decide one thing explicitly and write it into §24.3: does a LATER retraction in the pool retroactively affect an ALREADY-adopted delta? Recommendation: NO, and say so - the adoption was a lawful operator act at the time, the adopted delta is the operator's own claim now, and withdrawing it is the operator's own §14 retraction rather than an echo of the pool's. Anything else would make canonical history mutable by a sandbox, which is the property §24 exists to deny. But it must be STATED, because the alternative reading is defensible and silence would leave it to whoever implements next.

RAILS:
- The probe: a pool output struck inside the pool cannot be promoted; the refusal names the retraction.
- TRANSITIVE: strike, then counter-strike (which revives), and promotion SUCCEEDS again - survival is the live reading, not 'was ever negated'.
- An already-adopted delta is unaffected by a retraction landing in the pool afterwards (the decision above, railed).
- Unchanged: every existing promotion path stays green - `test/gateway/promotion.test.ts` with no assertion softened, idempotence and the erasure interaction (#111) intact.

CARE: capability + federation surface, and it changes what enters canonical history -> Myk's merge (P6).
title: Promotion checks PRESENCE, not SURVIVAL - a retracted pool output is adopted in the operator's own voice
